name: Enterprise
image_description: The hand diagram of the chiromancers shows the complete set of planetary qualities. Jupiter's nature in palmistry is to denote the achievement of enterprise and ambition, where full use is made of all talents available. The acorns are simply the seeds of potential.
personal: Jupiter in Aries people have an expansive drive to develop and improve life by taking on positions of leadership. Positive action and an enjoyment of physically doing things are a necessary part of their make-up. Jupiter/Aries types tend to "rush in where angels fear to tread," but all positive effort on their part is good for their well-being. Yes, these types are extremely impulsive, but with the beneficial planet Jupiter involved, they get more than their fair share of lucky breaks. Optimistic and self-assured socially, people with this planet/sign aspect are fortunate in sending out little signals of honest trustworthiness that make for plenty of popularity. They have the added quality of giving confidence to other people. The optimism of the people this card describes is based on the belief that the harsh old world around them will support their buoyant enthusiasm – and of course it does. They share this aspect with many actors, politicians and religious or philosophical leaders. Jupiter is a buoyant influence, and imparts to Aries the optimistic belief that all that one does will have a successful outcome. More often than not with this combination in play, the result can be just that.
reading: When this card occurs in a reading it signifies a successful consequence for ventures referred to in the question. The Enterprise card suggests that a bold and enterprising approach will be needed to achieve a satisfactory result.
events: Setting up a business; working for a foreign organization; using personality to improve one's status; enacting ideas for long-term success.

name: Status
image_description: A dignified couple, man and woman, are posed as if for a portrait. Above them, a crown hovers. The two lion-headed dragons are symbols of might and dignity. Below, elaborate brocades indicate riches.
personal: In developing and improving life, Jupiter/Taureans gain much benefit from money and material resources. They are aware of the energy these items can generate, and have the ability to expand their worth for themselves and others. The health of people with this planet/sign combination suffers, however, if their love of material comforts becomes too excessive. Though the Jupiter Taurean's ability to make money will be blessed at times by extreme good fortune, the people this card describes have an occasional impulsive Jupiterian urge to blow their good luck on crazy bouts of excess and extravagance. They are better when they involve themselves with money-making ventures that cater for other people's luxurious desires. This planet/sign aspect indicates a taste for artistic things, and good judgment of the true value of art objects as an investment. Jupiter Taureans are, in fact, extremely good at sensing the market for anything artistic, beautiful or luxurious – in whichever direction their talents lie, there is much movement of money. Negatively, those who place emphasis on worldly achievements run the risk of being judged on the basis of material possessions. The ability to give as easily and skillfully as one acquires is the key to striking the balance.
reading: When this card is drawn, it hints that one should be aware of priorities and question whether material gain is really most important at the moment.
events: Gaining pleasure from beautiful things, extravagant taste, a generous love of art; using money to cultivate powerful friends.

name: Bluff
image_description: A Minoan goddess holds two butterflies – or are they really axes? The hands show back and front, as does the professional conjurer performing a sleight-of-hand trick.
personal: The basic drive for Jupiter Geminians in improving life will always be to keep the mind active and informed. People with this combination have an intellectual curiosity that leads to continuing mental development at all stages of life. Often, they wish to be seen to know everything, and with their quick wit can collect enough information to bluff their way through any situation. Jupiter in Gemini people often achieve great knowledge and mental achievements without formal education, and this planet/sign combination can indicate the talented self-taught intellectual. Such people boost their experience with travel, and social relationships, which contribute to their mental expansion. Travel is beneficial for Jupiter Geminians, enriching the mind as well as relaxing the natural tension that accompanies their mental restlessness. Some form of meditation is of benefit when these hyperactive heads get dizzy. Contacts with close relatives are likely to be convivial for Jupiter Geminians. The people this card describes are good analysts of current thought, and perceptive social and political commentators.
reading: In a reading, this card indicates a need to throw oneself into an unfamiliar situation or challenge and use one's wits to bluff through the first stage. This is not as dishonest an approach as it sounds. A brain surgeon has to make the first operation as confidently as if it were the fiftieth.
events: A situation in which one has to impress others with knowledge; discussions about business or legal affairs; a lecture or talk to a group or society.

name: Speculation
image_description: Below, the Cancerian crab, its claws outstretched, touches the alchemical circle depicting the four elements that blend in universal creation. In the center an eye looks for opportunity. The king and queen signify marriage, tradition and stability.
personal: In order to expand and develop their lives, people with Jupiter in Cancer feel a need to keep and develop their strong sense of family roots and traditions. This desire to be around and care for others may spread into work for the community at large, which may be seen as an extension of the family. Jupiter Cancerians also benefit from exploring their talent for historical research or for expanding their understanding of the past. The early family upbringing of people with the Jupiter/Cancer combination has usually been supportive and comfortable, and so they try to pass these qualities on to those surrounding them. If not inheritors of material possessions, they certainly inherit strong family traditions – and most usually both. Often people with Jupiter in Cancer receive a family inheritance that may become the basis for establishing a family business. People with this planet/sign combination usually have an excellent feeling for acquiring property, and for improving its value once in their ownership. Their own home may, at some time, be used for gatherings of a religious, philosophical or educational nature.
reading: When this card is drawn during a reading, it encourages the questioner to look to his or her family for support and even financial help with regard to the question asked. The Speculation card is also a good indication of a successful outcome to an imminent property deal.
events: A legal situation involving the home, running a family business from home; exploiting an inheritance to the benefit of the family.

name: Publicity
image_description: An angel reaches and touches the stars. She is Publicity, carrying the flag and spreading fame far and wide. She circles the small globe of the earth containing the four elemental colors: red-fire, green-earth, yellow-air and blue-water.
personal: In order for Jupiter Leos to develop and grow, they constantly need to find themselves in situations in which they can put on an impressive show. Jupiter Leos think bigger than most, and so have to take the lead if they want to get things done. Local events that need creative imagination and lavish ideas are waiting for their special talents. Plenty of socializing gives people with this planet/sign combination ample chance to radiate their natural warmth, but if they can find a creative job, then so much the better. Their creativity finds good opportunity for appreciation in any of the arts, and their talents, whether professional or amateur, grow as they mature. With this placing of Jupiter, gambling will be a temptation, and some discipline may be useful to rein-in over-optimism in this area. The children of Jupiter Leos, if they have any, are likely to be a source of great pride, and parents will spare no expense on their education. There is likely to be at least one creatively talented and successful offspring.
reading: Because of the Leo link with children, when this card is drawn in a reading, it can indicate the encouragement of a child in a creative expression of their talents. This can manifest negatively as the archetypal over-pushy mother of the child star, living out her fantasies of fame through her progeny. Positively, it can refer to a nurturing and supportive parent.
events: Blowing one's own trumpet; the attraction of some kind of fame or attention, taking a leading role in a local activity; a prominent member of the church or similar organization.

name: Patience
image_description: A gentlewoman strolls in her garden. She wears an elaborately and delicately embroidered dress. The ox and the lion look away from each other in peace. The classical heads are earth goddesses Circe and Gaia.
personal: Jupiter Virgos expand their lives most when they can exploit their outstanding analytical and critical sense to the full. Excelling at detailed work, the talents of people with this planet/sign combination achieve most in some kind of specialist activity. Philosophically, people with this aspect are able to sift the truth from most arguments, but may have to watch their over-critical attitude to others who are less mentally endowed. Health and attention to diet is important to the well-being of Jupiter Virgoans. They have an innate understanding that mental and emotional states have a great deal to do with health, and demonstrate an ability to help others in this area. Consequently, Jupiter Virgos have the potential for developing abilities in healing, this being more likely within the spheres of complementary medicine or faith-healing. With the expansive planet Jupiter in the very modest and conscientious sign of Virgo, there is inevitably a conflict, and this may reveal itself as an excessive concentration on detailed work to the detriment of health. It also manifests as unassuming devotion to duty that is taken to extremes, or may show in obsessive behavior with regard to things such as cleanliness or tidiness.
reading: When this card appears in a reading, it may show that the questioner should not be afraid to ask for assistance with a project.
events: Acting as secretary for a social or self-help group; reorganizing the methods and system of an organization; spending time helping someone with a learning difficulty.

name: Negotiation
image_description: In a field before a small town stands a dark maiden from a distant land, her hands raised to provide a link between Sun and Moon, earth, emotions, creativity and possessions.
personal: People with Jupiter in Libra expand their lives through frequent interaction with others, gaining much from co-operative work and close personal relationships. Their strong concern for justice and moral principles gives these personality types useful diplomatic skills. Jupiter Librans have a talent for gentle persuasion that would be fulfilling and effective in social and charitable work of some kind. Desire to please others can, however, sometimes lead this personality to try to be all things to all people: often, people with this aspect promise more than they can deliver in order to please, and end up achieving the opposite result. This card usually indicates a strong and lasting marriage or relationship, and Jupiter Librans are also usually lucky enough to find themselves in the satisfying position of acquiring much material and spiritual well-being. Negatively, expansive Jupiter Librans need to take care not to get involved with grandiose schemes and speculative partnerships – they might wreck the good things they already have. The optimistic outlook Jupiter encourages may push Librans into unhelpful indecision, seeing equal value in every possibility.
reading: In a reading, the Negotiation card indicates that the time has come to make a decision, and that there is a need to consider the pros and cons of a situation to make sure the answer is acceptable to both parties.
events: Learning a language for a specific project; taking the chair at a meeting, being spokesperson for someone unjustly accused; negotiating a business deal; marriage guidance.

name: Manipulation
image_description: A powerful and magnetic priest or shaman stares penetratingly at the viewer. Above is an eight-pointed star representing the eighth house Scorpio, and on the headdress three six-pointed stars represent wisdom and enlightenment.
personal: Jupiter Scorpios expand and develop their lives through deep and passionate interests in psychology and mysticism, by which they explore the hidden secrets of life. The Jupiter Scorpio's passion for life's deeper subjects could develop into such things as life after death, reincarnation or spiritualism. Legal work or business may attract them, and they could gain from other people's finances, either through assistance or speculation. The ability to see and help develop other people's talents gives the Jupiter/Scorpio personality good possibilities in counseling or some kind of management work. People with this planet/sign aspect have the ability to discover other people's secrets – whether for altruistic or selfish reasons – and their magnetic and penetrating powers can be used almost hypnotically to achieve their objectives. Positively, Jupiter Scorpios could find themselves with the responsibility for helping others with their deepest problems, and this planet/sign combination can produce the devoted entrepreneur who supports and encourages prodigies with unfailing work. On the other hand, these are people who can persuade and seduce with their enormous charisma, leading others into whatever they desire.
reading: In a reading, this card may warn that one is following a false or manipulative person. Scorpio's natural rulership of the Eighth House shows a benefit from an inheritance or heirloom.
events: A difficult legal problem; persuasive promotion or propaganda for a person or project; detective work to prise out someone's secret.

name: Principle
image_description: Ruins represent the establishment of earlier times. The mitre is a symbol of spiritual power across centuries and countries. The constellation Noah's Dove with its prominent star Phact is ascribed to the nature of Jupiter and Saturn by Ptolemy.
personal: With Jupiter in its own sign of Sagittarius, we see the perfect combination of planet and sign. For the lucky people with this aspect, the most satisfying way to enrich life is through broad, adventurous travel, both physically and mentally. Studying or living for a period within another culture is fulfilling for people described by this card, and a major philosophy or religion will form an important part of life, although this may manifest as psychology or politics. Jupiter Sagittarians can also be concerned with publishing, lecturing and teaching, but always connected with a strong set of ethics. These interests produce a far-sightedness that can manifest as "seeing into the future." Jupiter Sagittarians have a strong desire to acquire as much education as possible and take advantage of opportunities for involvement in mature studies – which can lead on to distinguished achievements. They are also, however, susceptible to involvement in extremist religious or philosophical beliefs. On the negative side, it is possible that people with this planet/sign combination may seek to impose their concepts on those around them to the extent that they develop a self-righteous attitude that rides roughshod over other views.
reading: When this card appears in a reading, it will raise a matter of principle or life-philosophy that is particularly related to the question.
events: Planning a grand tour or world trip; being completely immersed in a new philosophy or religion, conversion, the need to persuade others of a philosophy.

name: Control
image_description: The creator god Zeus/Jupiter, feet resting on the world, looks down at his work. Cherubim carry the canopy of the sky above the verdant earth. Below, the ouroboros, a serpent with its tail in its mouth, guards the earth, a symbol of time, eternity and the universe.
personal: People with Jupiter in Capricorn feel an ever-demanding ambition to improve life and status by taking on positions of responsibility. They find fruitful and satisfying areas of life in which they are able to exercise care and judgment in decision-making. Achievement is important for Jupiter/Capricorn well-being, and they aspire to positions of authority. Their achievements lead people with this planet/sign combination to take on a prominent position in the community – they have the potential to become a pillar of society – and a strong ethical or religious sense will be important to them, and to other people's image of them. This card reveals excellent prospects for public office and positions of power, however modest. There is always present a danger that ambition can interfere with home and family life, and this may become an area of conflict. The negative side of this card can be a stubborn sticking to the letter of the law in situations that demand a more flexible approach. This rigid attitude taken to extremes can also show as miserliness in small matters alongside excessive extravagance in large ones.
reading: When this card appears in a reading, it often indicates a need to take on full responsibility for one's current position and apply control in order to extricate oneself from a difficult situation.
events: Taking on a responsible position in an organization; achieving recognition for lengthy and patient work; a politician or business tycoon, a prominent figure in the religious community.

name: Innovation
image_description: Seated on a strange plant-stool, a female figure in unusual striped attire holds a whip-like instrument attached to a ball containing a nine-petaled flower. At the four corners are ankhs; three are the same shape; the fourth is different.
personal: In developing and enhancing life, Jupiter Aquarians gain a great deal through involvement with reforms, social organizations and good causes. The interests of their fellow humans are important to people with this planet/sign combination, and a code of ethics that is universal has great appeal. Unusual philosophies, religions or occult science will prove an attraction throughout life, since Jupiter Aquarians are broad-minded and always open to new ideas. Most of their life goals will be achieved with friends and in group activities as their concern for humanity at large tends to increase their popularity. The Jupiter/Aquarius interest in new ways of seeing and community activities enables people with this combination to carry out large and constructive endeavors with success. These may be of a charitable or humanitarian nature, carried out in the name of religious, educational or fraternal organizations. The negative side of this planet/sign combination can manifest as excessive changeability and eccentric behavior, to the extent that friends and partners learn not to rely on Jupiter Aquarians. Their minds can be so inventive and scattered that they diffuse their potential wastefully.
reading: In a reading, this card can indicate a totally new approach to a problem. It may involve the help of clever and talented friends as Aquarius rules the Eleventh House of friendship.
events: Work on an original idea, being involved with a new invention; an avant-garde group formed to benefit or educate others, using ingenuity to help the less fortunate.

name: Seduction
image_description: In the center, the first woman Eve is plucking the apple from the Tree of Knowledge. Before her is the constellation Cassiopeia, associated by Kabalists with the second Tarot trump, The High Priestess. Heart and flowers represent the mundane view of love.
personal: Jupiter is the traditional ruler of Pisces, and people with this planet/sign position develop and improve life by championing the underdog and those less fortunate than themselves. Care, as always with the sympathetic Piscean, needs to be taken not to be undiscriminating in this respect, as people with this aspect could be susceptible to others taking advantage. Jupiter Pisceans are extremely sensitive to those around them, and may even have some psychic ability. Whether they do or not, some mystical or higher path will play an important part in life. They develop a strong interest in the inner spiritual path through seclusion and meditation, and mysticism and religious thought will be important. The emotional satisfaction that Jupiter/Pisces people derive from helping others may be a very deep and fulfilling experience, and though their work may be behind the scenes, it will be appreciated. People this card describes are, however, vulnerable to deception from those who seek to take advantage of their caring and sensitive nature. In its most negative aspect, this combination indicates people whose sensitivity to others and desire to please leads them into being seduced by any and every exploiter.
reading: This card is a warning that everything may not be as it appears and that someone is being seduced for ulterior motives.
events: Becoming the victim of a hard-luck story; sacrificing a great deal for someone who is loved; having the intuition and ability to sense what another person wants.
